recommendation: Extend spec-gate's pre-judge mechanical check with a structural per-scenario assertion-count/step-identity diff (not just a raw line-diff) so an Edit-tool insert that silently reassigns an existing step onto the wrong (adjacent/newly-added) scenario is caught mechanically before the cold judge. This is a distinct failure mode from D1's use-case-row coverage check (which catches a use case with NO backing scenario) — here a frozen scenario already HAD its backing assertion and an in-session mechanical edit orphaned it onto a different scenario, invisible to a line-oriented diff because both scenarios' line counts stayed plausible.
evidence: d1-pre-judge-semantic-checks.log.jsonl seq2 (correction-kind: judge-iteration, cause: spec-feature-contradiction): the frozen scenario "every touched reference resolving" lost its "And the gate spawns the cold judge" assertion — an Edit-tool insert reassigned the line onto the newly-added last scenario by context-line reassignment; caught only by the cold spec-judge on round 1, not by any pre-judge mechanical check (including D1's own newly-shipped use-case-row check, since coverage nominally still existed, just on the wrong scenario)
